pattern: Only the two most recent rolls constrain the next one (coprime with the last, different from both), so a DP over ordered pairs of recent values counts all valid sequences modulo 1e9+7.
hints: Whether a roll is legal depends only on the previous roll (gcd condition) and the one before that (the gap-of-2 rule).
Track the number of valid sequences ending in each ordered pair (second-last, last) of dice faces.
Transition each pair (a, b) to (b, c) whenever c differs from a and b and gcd(b, c) equals 1, summing counts modulo 1e9+7; handle n=1 and n=2 as base cases.

edge_inputs.description: small n exercising the gap rule

stress.description: random n across the valid range
stress.note: verifies the linear DP and modulo at scale
